title: GateSummaryOutput
description: Structured output produced by the gate-summary LLM call. Has both the human-facing markdown prose (rendered as-is) and a machine-readable concerns array (drives the triage panel). Concerns are NEVER parsed back out of summary_md — the FE reads the array directly. The contract makes the LLM responsible for keeping the two representations in sync within a single call.

summary_md.description: Full human-readable summary in markdown: the four required sections (Summary / What was done / Concerns / Recommendation) in the language the ticket+brief are written in. Rendered as-is in the gate decision panel; the FE never parses this string.

concerns.description: Structured copy of every concern surfaced in the markdown's `## 注意点 / Concerns` section. Order and count MUST match what summary_md shows so the human reading the prose sees the same set the triage panel asks them to classify. Empty array when there are no concerns (= the `## Concerns` section says "特になし" / "None").

recommendation.description: Top-level recommendation the LLM ended on. The PdM may override; this is recorded as the LLM's read at the moment of summary generation. Must match the verdict expressed in the markdown's Recommendation section.

text.description: Plain-text body of the concern, without source-tag suffix or markdown bullet marker. Equivalent prose to the markdown bullet's content.

severity.description: Severity assigned by the aggregator (`plan_review.aggregate` / `code_quality_review.aggregate`). Omitted when the concern is brand-new at this gate (= didn't come from a prior aggregate).

status.description: How an earlier stage already handled this concern. `accepted` = aggregator decided non-blocking, `deferred` = punted to a follow-up ticket, `noted` = just observation, `new` = surfaced first at this gate. Omitted is equivalent to `new`.

source_node.description: Engine node id where the concern was first raised (e.g. `plan_review.aggregate` / `code_quality_review.aggregate`). Omitted when the concern is brand-new at this gate.
